After a detailed comparison against the official template files (Back_Me_Up_Letter_Template.docx and the 2024 Letterhead NPC.docx), there are a number of discrepancies that need to be corrected before this can be signed off. I want to be clear — these are not stylistic preferences; they are documented requirements in the source files themselves.

Please action the following:

1. BODY FONT — CRITICAL
The letterhead template explicitly specifies Segoe UI Light for all body text. The current output is rendering in what appears to be Calibri or Arial. This must be updated across the entire letter body.

2. HEADING / ADDRESSEE FONT — CRITICAL
The template specifies Segoe UI (regular, bold) for the attention/RE line and addressee block. The current output does not differentiate this from body text. Please apply the correct font weight.

3. MEMBER NAME NOT BOLDED — CRITICAL
Per the template, [Member Name] is explicitly bolded. In the generated letter, "Ilse-Mari Nelson" appears in plain text in both the opening paragraph and the closing support sentence. Both instances must be bold.

4. DESIGNATION NOT BOLDED — CRITICAL
The template bolds the designation name. "Chartered Business Accountant in Practice (Chartered BAP)" must render in bold in the opening paragraph.

5. VALIDITY LINE NOT ITALICISED — CRITICAL
The line "This letter is valid for 30 days from the date of issue." is marked as italic in the template. The current output renders it in upright regular text. This must be italicised.

6. BULLET LIST LABELS NOT BOLDED
Each bullet point has a category label (Smart:, Loyal:, Ethical and Accountable:, Professionally Regulated:). Per the template, these labels must be bold, with the description text following in regular weight. Currently both are the same weight.

ticket.summary: System Issue – Incorrect Automatic Update of Membership Status
ticket.description: Hi IT Team, I hope you’re well. We are currently having an issue where the system automatically updates a member’s profile to Good Standing, even though the member does not fully meet the requirements for that status. To give context: In one case, the system updated the member’s status to Good Standing after a debit order setup, despite the member still having outstanding CPD hours and pending declarations. In another case, the member made a full payment while their profile was still technically suspended, but the system still marked them as Good Standing. In both cases, the team manually change the profile back to Not in Good Standing to ensure accuracy. It seems the system may currently be recognising payment or debit order setup as the sole condition for Good Standing, without validating the other compliance requirements (CPDs, declarations, and APL/PRA status). Please could you investigate and confirm if this is a broader system issue, and whether additional validation rules may need to be reviewed. I’ve attached the examples shared by my team for your reference. ticket.description:
Hi IT team,

I hope you are well. I am writing to bring a system discrepancy to your attention that we have encountered on the ECMS platform and would like to request an urgent investigation.

Below is a summary of what we have observed:

A member CIBA202410-27075 submitted their membership application and upon reviewing the exported ECMS list, the application is reflected as "Complete." It was our understanding that applications marked as "Complete" on ECMS are processed as auto-approvals.

However, upon reviewing the member's profile, we noted the following concerns:

• The member made payment on 31 March 2026 yet the exported list indicates the "Compete" status on the 29th of March
• No supporting documents have been uploaded by the member (CV and ID)
• The application status is marked as "Complete"
• The profile status is marked as "Pending"
• Despite the above, the product is active and the membership letter is available and accessible

Actions taken on our end:
• We have deactivated the product to prevent the member from accessing the membership letter until all required documents have been submitted, reviewed, and approved
• We have updated the profile from Pending to Active as we have noted the application fee on the profile

We are concerned that this may not be an isolated case and we are not certain how many profiles may have been affected by this discrepancy. Please find attached the relevant screenshots for your reference to assist with the investigation.

Kindly investigate and advise at your earliest convenience.

Thank you for your assistance.

Kind regards,
Christinah

value.summary: Dev request — V2 Member Profile: expose product layer, add PPC card, fix member reporting, clean up Status picklist

ticket.summary: Dev request — V2 Member Profile: expose product layer, add PPC card, fix member reporting, clean up Status picklist
ticket.description: Attachment: v2-member-mockup.htmlHi Team,Logging a development request for V2 covering the View Member page, Member Search, member reporting, and a cleanup of our Status picklist. A working mockup is attached (open in a browser; default view is Member Search — click View Profile on Chantelle Booyens' card to see the proposed View Member layout).Background — why this mattersV1 let staff see at a glance a member's active designation(s), and — critically — let us pull reports that included suspended and cancelled members with full membership detail. That reporting capability is what management, finance, and compliance rely on for member analytics, revenue reporting, AR reconciliation, and regulatory submissions.V2 is product-driven (every membership, designation, licence, club and CPD pack is a product with a validity window in ECMS), but neither the profile screen nor the reporting layer currently surfaces the product data cleanly. Two consequences:First, on-screen we can't easily see what products a member holds, when they expire, or who's servicing them — this slows down day-to-day member support.Second, and more serious, we cannot produce a reliable "Member × Product × Status × Validity" report that includes suspended, cancelled, or non-renewed members. That blocks several monthly/quarterly processes: the AR age analysis, the IFRS 9 ECL calculation, designation-holder counts submitted to regulators, CPD compliance reporting, and revenue recognition tied to product validity. No existing fields on the View Member page should be removed. Everything below is additive or a cleanup of an existing picklist.Scope of changes1. View Member — new "Latest Products" section (Priority: High) A card below "Member Information" listing every product on the member's account with columns: Product Type tag (Membership / Designation / Licence / Club / CPD Pack), Product name, Product code, Validity period (from → to), Status pill, Last updated (date + user). Must show all products including Cancelled and Suspended so we retain reporting visibility. Include "Product History" button for full timeline and "+ Add Product" for manual additions.2. View Member — new "Servicing PPC" section (Priority: High) A card showing the PPC assigned to this member: name, role, region, email, phone, assignment date. Needs a "Reassign" action, plus "Email PPC" and "Log Interaction" quick buttons. The PPC is a person record — we'll need a link-table between Member and PPC with an effective date.3. View Member — new "Compliance Snapshot" section (Priority: Medium) Four-tile summary for the current compliance cycle: CPD Hours (actual / required), Annual Declaration (status + submission date), Next Renewal date, Outstanding Balance. Pulls from existing data — no new capture, just surfacing.4. Member Search — product-layer results (Priority: High) Search results cards should show member identity (name, member code, legacy code, status, compliance), the PPC chip on the right, and a Latest Products block inline (same product-row format as the profile), plus a compliance strip footer. Add PPC to the filter bar. This replaces the current thin result row.5. Member Reporting & Data Extract — fix the reporting gap (Priority: HIGH / blocker for finance)This is the change that unblocks the business case. Two deliverables:5a. A canonical "Member × Product" report/view — a reportable dataset (database view, Power BI model, or report template — whichever fits your stack) that joins User → Membership → Designations → Licences → Products → Validity Periods → Status → Cancellation Reason → Last Updated → PPC. Must include all member states (Active, Pending, Suspended, Cancellation Requested, Cancelled). Must be filterable by status, product type, validity period, PPC, and billing cycle. This becomes the single source of truth for member analytics and replaces the multiple manual extracts we run today.5b. Export to Excel / CSV directly from Member Search results and from the View Member product list, respecting current filters. Needed so finance and compliance can pull ad-hoc views without raising a data request each time.Suspended and cancelled members must appear in both outputs with full product history (not filtered out by default). A "Status = Active only" toggle can hide them for user-facing screens, but the underlying data model must retain them.6. Status picklist cleanup (Priority: Medium — do in same release) Current list has nine values with duplicates and a reason-as-status problem. Proposed final set:Active — keepPending Activation — rename from plain "Pending"Pending Documents — keepPending Payment — keepCancellation Requested — keepCancellation Approved — rename from "Cancellation Processed" (means approved, running out paid validity)Cancelled — keepSuspended — keepRetire "Cancelled Dormant Account" as a status — it's a reason, not a state. Move "Dormant Account" to the existing Cancellation Reason picklist alongside Non-renewal, Voluntary, Deceased, Disciplinary, Migrated, Upgraded, Other.Also please clarify the relationship between Status = Suspended and Suspension Status = Yes/No — today both exist and can contradict each other. Recommendation: drop the separate Suspension Status boolean and make Suspended a value in the Status column, with a new Suspension Reason picklist (CPD Non-compliance, Outstanding Balance, Failed Declaration, Disciplinary).Acceptance criteriaA suspended or cancelled member's products must still be visible on View Member and in Member Search — not filtered out by default. The canonical Member × Product report must return every member regardless of status, with full product history and correct validity windows. Excel export from search and profile must include all visible columns plus hidden metadata (product code, last updated by, cancellation reason). Latest Products must show last updated by (user or system.renewal / system.cpd). PPC assignment must persist across sessions and be searchable. Status picklist migration must map all existing records to the new values with no data loss — propose a mapping spreadsheet before the migration runs.Nice to have (separate tickets, not blocking)Product history timeline view. Bulk PPC reassignment. CPD progress bar on the snapshot tile. Hover tooltip on each Status pill explaining the rule. Scheduled email export of the Member × Product report on a monthly cadence.Happy to walk through this on a call if easier — the mockup captures the intent better than words, and I can demo the specific reports we're currently stitching together manually. Let me know your estimate and we'll prioritise into a release. Kr, Chantelle BooyensChief Financial Officercbooyens@myciba.orgt: +2712 643 1800c: +2776 357 6314www.myciba.orgCape Town  | Pretoria  | Windhoek        .The information and/or opinions contained in this email is of a general nature and may in certain circumstances be subject to misinterpretation.
